description_long: This is a mid-rise apartment building with a rectangular footprint and a dark, sloped roof. The facade features a repetitive arrangement of windows and balconies, with the longer sides displaying multiple stacked balconies protected by metal or glass railings. The exterior walls are clad in a muted, beige-brown material, likely concrete or stucco, with some weathering visible. The roof has several small, boxy structures, possibly chimneys or ventilation shafts. The building appears to have five stories, with each floor having a consistent window and balcony pattern. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal ornamentation, typical of post-war or Soviet-era residential architecture.
description: A five-story rectangular apartment building with a dark sloped roof, beige-brown facade, and rows of balconies on the long sides. The design is functional and repetitive, with minimal decorative elements.
description_view_0: The narrow end of the building shows a plain facade with evenly spaced windows and a sloped roof with several small rooftop structures.
description_view_1: The long side reveals multiple floors with regularly spaced balconies and windows, highlighting the building's repetitive residential layout.
description_view_2: Similar to view 0, this end view displays the plain, windowed facade and the sloped roof, mirroring the opposite side.
description_view_3: The opposite long side also features stacked balconies and windows, with subtle lighting at the base, emphasizing the building's residential function.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are symmetrically arranged in vertical and horizontal rows, with consistent spacing and alignment across all floors. Balconies are grouped along the long sides, each corresponding to a window bay.
